chapter_summary: Travelling through Samaria, Jesus stops at Jacob's well and speaks with a Samaritan woman who comes alone at midday. He offers her 'living water', reveals He knows her whole life, and declares Himself the Messiah. She runs to tell her whole town; many believe. Jesus stays two days. Then He heals a royal official's dying son from a distance — the man believes and his whole household with him.
main_lesson: Jesus offers living water — His love and life that satisfies like nothing else can. And He knows us completely and loves us anyway.

memory_verse.kjv: But whosoever drinketh of the water that I shall give him shall never thirst; but the water that I shall give him shall be in him a well of water springing up into everlasting life.
memory_verse.little_bible: Jesus said: 'Whoever drinks the water I give will never be thirsty again! My water becomes a spring inside them — welling up into life that lasts forever!'

parent_guide: The Samaritan woman is one of the most powerful encounters in the Gospels — a person marked by social rejection, ethnic prejudice, and personal shame, to whom Jesus reveals Himself as the Messiah. Key themes: Jesus crosses barriers; He knows us completely yet loves us fully; true worship is 'in spirit and in truth' (v23-24); one person's testimony brought a whole community to faith. The healing of the official's son shows that Jesus' word alone is enough — no touch, no travel needed.
application_for_children: We can always talk to Jesus — no matter who we are or what we've done. He knows everything about us and still wants to be our friend.

verses.kjv: When therefore the LORD knew how the Pharisees had heard that Jesus made and baptized more disciples than John,
verses.little_bible: The Pharisees — the religious leaders — had heard that Jesus was making and baptising more followers than John the Baptist.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The religious leaders heard that Jesus had more followers than John.
verses.meaning: Jesus' growing influence was attracting notice from the Pharisees. This would eventually lead to conflict. Jesus, knowing this, chose to move.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus knew what the leaders were thinking.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You always know what is happening around me. Help me to trust Your timing and wisdom.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the Pharisees were paying close attention to how many followers Jesus had?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus was gaining attention that made powerful people uncomfortable. Can following God ever bring the wrong kind of attention? How do we handle that?
verses.do_it_today: Ask God to give you wisdom today to know when to move forward and when to step back — just like Jesus did.
verses.illustration_prompt: Pharisees in fine religious robes huddled together, whispering and pointing toward a large crowd following Jesus in the distance. Concern and suspicion on their faces.

verses.kjv: (Though Jesus himself baptized not, but his disciples,)
verses.little_bible: (Note: Jesus Himself did not do the baptising — His disciples did it on His behalf.)
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus did not baptise people Himself — His disciples did.
verses.meaning: A clarifying note — it was the disciples who were baptising, acting under Jesus' authority. Jesus' work was not diminished; He empowered others to carry it out.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus worked through His disciples.
verses.prayer: Jesus, work through me too. Use me to do Your work in the world.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus gave His disciples the work of baptising. What work do you think Jesus wants to do through His followers today?
verses.family_discussion: God works through ordinary people — like Jesus' disciples. How might God want to work through your family?
verses.do_it_today: Do one thing today as an act of service for God — knowing He can work through you.
verses.illustration_prompt: A disciple gently lowering someone into a river while Jesus watches from the bank, smiling in approval. The work of the kingdom carried out through willing hands.

verses.kjv: He left Judaea, and departed again into Galilee.
verses.little_bible: So Jesus left Judaea and headed back toward Galilee.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus left and headed back toward Galilee.
verses.meaning: Jesus chose not to stay where tension was building. He was always moving with purpose — not reacting, not retreating, but advancing according to God's plan.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus moved with purpose.
verses.prayer: God, help me not to run from problems, but to move with Your purpose and timing.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus chose to leave instead of staying to confront the Pharisees. Is it always right to stay and fight?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus often chose to move on rather than get drawn into conflict. What can we learn from that about picking our battles?
verses.do_it_today: Is there a situation you need to leave gracefully instead of staying and creating conflict? Ask God for wisdom.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus and His disciples walking along a dusty road, heading north. Purposeful strides, quiet resolve. A new horizon ahead.

verses.kjv: And he must needs go through Samaria.
verses.little_bible: The route took Him through Samaria — and He had to go that way.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus had to travel through Samaria.
verses.meaning: Devout Jews would often take a longer route to avoid Samaria. Jesus did not. The word 'must' suggests a divine appointment was waiting — a woman who needed to meet her Saviour.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus had to go — someone was waiting.
verses.prayer: Jesus, send me where You need me to go — even places others avoid.
verses.discussion_question: Most people avoided going through Samaria. Why do you think Jesus chose to go through it?
verses.family_discussion: There are people and places others avoid. How does Jesus challenge us to go toward those people instead of around them?
verses.do_it_today: Think of someone your circle tends to overlook or avoid. Take one step toward them today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A road map with two routes: a long way around Samaria, and a straight path right through it. Jesus' sandalled feet stepping onto the shorter, less-travelled path.

verses.kjv: Then cometh he to a city of Samaria, which is called Sychar, near to the parcel of ground that Jacob gave to his son Joseph.
verses.little_bible: He came to a Samaritan town called Sychar, near the land that the patriarch Jacob had given to his son Joseph long ago.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus came to a Samaritan town called Sychar, near land that Jacob had given to Joseph.
verses.meaning: The narrator roots this encounter in deep history — the very land where Israel's story was shaped. Jesus was not just passing through; He was stepping into the story of God's people.
verses.memory_phrase: God's story has deep roots.
verses.prayer: God, remind me that I am part of Your big, long story — from the beginning until now.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the Bible mentions that this land came from Jacob? What does that tell us about how God's story connects through history?
verses.family_discussion: Your family is part of God's long story too. What chapters has God written in your family's history?
verses.do_it_today: Thank God for the long history of His faithfulness — and that He has included you in it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A Samaritan hillside town. In the foreground, an ancient well. In the background, rolling hills. Sunlight suggesting history, depth, and holy significance.

verses.kjv: Now Jacob's well was there. Jesus therefore, being wearied with his journey, sat thus on the well: and it was about the sixth hour.
verses.little_bible: Jacob's famous well was right there. Jesus was tired from the long journey, so He sat down beside the well to rest. It was around midday.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus was tired from walking. He sat down at a well to rest. It was noon.
verses.meaning: Jesus was genuinely human — tired, dusty, thirsty. He did not come as a distant deity; He came fully into our world. And in His tiredness, He was still ready to give.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus was tired — and still willing to give.
verses.prayer: Jesus, even when I am tired, help me to be ready to give to others — just as You were.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus was tired from a long journey. Does it surprise you that the Son of God could be tired? What does that tell you about Him?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus knows what it feels like to be tired and worn out. How does that change how you bring your exhaustion to Him?
verses.do_it_today: Next time you are tired today, bring it to Jesus — He understands exactly how you feel.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus sitting on the stone rim of an ancient well, sandals dusty, resting. Midday sun high overhead. A look of genuine weariness on His face — fully human.

verses.kjv: There cometh a woman of Samaria to draw water: Jesus saith unto her, Give me to drink.
verses.little_bible: A Samaritan woman came to the well to draw water. Jesus said to her: 'Please, will you give me a drink?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A woman came to get water. Jesus said: 'Will you give me a drink?'
verses.meaning: Jesus initiated. He crossed barriers — Jew to Samaritan, man to woman — and began with a simple, genuine request. He asked her for something. That was radical.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus reached out first.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You that You always reach out first — to me, and to everyone who feels alone.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus asked the woman for a drink. Why was that such a surprising and kind thing to do?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus crossed a big social barrier to talk to this woman. Are there barriers — age, background, race — that stop us from talking to people we should reach?
verses.do_it_today: Say hello today to someone you would normally walk past or ignore.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus sitting at the well, looking up with a gentle, open expression at a woman approaching with a water jar. A warm, surprised look on her face — she did not expect to be spoken to.

verses.kjv: (For his disciples were gone away unto the city to buy meat.)
verses.little_bible: (His disciples had gone into town to buy some food, which is why He was alone at the well.)
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The disciples had gone to buy food, so Jesus was alone.
verses.meaning: God arranged this moment carefully — Jesus alone, the woman coming at midday. The meeting was no accident. The disciples' absence was part of the plan.
verses.memory_phrase: God arranges moments for us to meet people.
verses.prayer: God, help me notice the 'no accident' moments You arrange in my day — and be present for them.
verses.discussion_question: Was it an accident that Jesus was alone when the woman arrived? What does that tell us about how God works?
verses.family_discussion: God places people in our path on purpose. Can your family think of someone God has placed in your lives recently?
verses.do_it_today: Be present and attentive today — someone God has placed near you might need a conversation.
verses.illustration_prompt: An empty road leading into town with dust settling behind the disciples who have just left. Jesus alone at the well, sitting quietly. A divine setup in an ordinary moment.

verses.kjv: Then saith the woman of Samaria unto him, How is it that thou, being a Jew, askest drink of me, which am a woman of Samaria? for the Jews have no dealings with the Samaritans.
verses.little_bible: The Samaritan woman was surprised. She said: 'You are a Jew — why are You asking me for a drink? I am a Samaritan woman! Jews do not usually talk to Samaritans.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The woman was surprised: 'You are Jewish! Why are you talking to me?'
verses.meaning: She knew the rules — Jews and Samaritans did not mix. Jesus had already broken them. Her surprise shows how unexpected His kindness was.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus broke barriers with kindness.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me to break barriers with kindness — like You did.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus crossed two big barriers here — he was Jewish and she was Samaritan, and he was a man talking to a woman alone. Why do you think He did not let those rules stop Him?
verses.family_discussion: Society often creates rules about who we should and should not talk to. What barriers does Jesus challenge us to cross?
verses.do_it_today: Talk to someone today who is different from you — different background, age, or experience.
verses.illustration_prompt: The woman with a water jar looking at Jesus with wide, surprised eyes. He looks calm and kind. Two worlds colliding in an unexpected conversation.

verses.kjv: Jesus answered and said unto her, If thou knewest the gift of God, and who it is that saith to thee, Give me to drink; thou wouldest have asked of him, and he would have given thee living water.
verses.little_bible: Jesus answered: 'If you only knew the gift God is offering — and who it is who is speaking to you right now — you would have asked Me, and I would have given you living water.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'If you knew who I am, you would ask Me for living water.'
verses.meaning: Jesus gently redirected the conversation from her question to the bigger truth. She was focused on who He was socially; He was focused on who He was eternally — and what He could give.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus offers a gift beyond what we expect.
verses.prayer: Jesus, open my eyes to see the gift You are offering me right now.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus said 'if you only knew' — the woman didn't realise who she was talking to. What difference does it make when we realise who Jesus really is?
verses.family_discussion: The woman came for well water — Jesus was offering living water. Are there moments when we settle for less than what Jesus really wants to give us?
verses.do_it_today: Come to Jesus today and ask — not just for the ordinary, but for the living water He promises.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus gesturing with open hands, looking at the woman with an expression of patient invitation. Light from above, the well behind Him. She leans in slightly, beginning to listen.

verses.kjv: The woman saith unto him, Sir, thou hast nothing to draw with, and the well is deep: from whence then hast thou that living water?
verses.little_bible: The woman replied: 'Sir, You have no bucket and the well is deep. Where would You get this living water?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The woman asked: 'You have no bucket! How would You get this water?'
verses.meaning: She heard literally, as Nicodemus had done. But her question, while earthy, was honest and open — exactly the kind of question Jesus works with.
verses.memory_phrase: Ask your honest questions. Jesus can work with them.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I have questions I do not fully understand. Please help me — like You helped this woman.
verses.discussion_question: The woman heard Jesus literally, about the bucket. But she kept talking to Him. Why is it good to keep talking to Jesus even when we are confused?
verses.family_discussion: We sometimes misunderstand what God is saying. That is okay. What matters is that we stay in the conversation. How does your family handle confusion about God?
verses.do_it_today: Bring a question or confusion about God to Him in prayer today. Keep the conversation going.
verses.illustration_prompt: The woman looking down at the well with a puzzled expression, holding her empty jar. Jesus sitting above, a quiet smile on His face — ready to explain something much bigger.

verses.kjv: Art thou greater than our father Jacob, which gave us the well, and drank thereof himself, and his children, and his cattle?
verses.little_bible: She continued: 'Are You greater than our ancestor Jacob? He gave us this well and drank from it himself — along with all his family and his animals!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: She asked: 'Are You greater than Jacob, who gave us this well?'
verses.meaning: She challenged Jesus with history and heritage. The well was a precious legacy. She was asking: who are You to offer something better? — and Jesus was about to answer.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus is greater than the greatest person in history.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I know You are greater than anyone who has ever lived. Help me to trust You above all others.
verses.discussion_question: The woman compared Jesus to Jacob — a great ancestor. Who do people today compare Jesus to? And what is the answer?
verses.family_discussion: People often compare Jesus to great historical figures. What makes Jesus uniquely greater than any other person?
verses.do_it_today: Name one person you have always greatly admired. Now think: how much greater is Jesus than even them?
verses.illustration_prompt: The woman gesturing proudly to the ancient well, speaking of Jacob. Jesus listening with calm confidence — knowing exactly who He is and what He can do.

verses.kjv: Jesus answered and said unto her, Whosoever drinketh of this water shall thirst again:
verses.little_bible: Jesus answered: 'Anyone who drinks this water will get thirsty again.',
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Everyone who drinks this water will be thirsty again.'
verses.meaning: Everything the world offers — even the best things, like Jacob's well — only satisfies temporarily. Jesus was building to the contrast.
verses.memory_phrase: The world's water always runs out.
verses.prayer: Jesus, remind me that nothing in this world truly satisfies — only You do.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever gotten something you really wanted and then found that you wanted more? What does that tell you about the things of this world?
verses.family_discussion: What are the 'wells' our family runs to when we are thirsty — for comfort, meaning, or happiness? Do they truly satisfy?
verses.do_it_today: Notice today when you reach for something other than Jesus to fill a need. Bring that thirst to Him instead.
verses.illustration_prompt: A person drinking from a well, satisfied for a moment — then, in the next frame, thirsty again. A recurring cycle that leaves them always returning, never truly full.

verses.kjv: But whosoever drinketh of the water that I shall give him shall never thirst; but the water that I shall give him shall be in him a well of water springing up into everlasting life.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'But whoever drinks the water I give will never be thirsty again! The water I give becomes a spring inside them — welling up into life that lasts forever!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'My water makes you never thirsty again. It becomes a spring inside you forever!'
verses.meaning: This is the heart of the conversation. Jesus offers not just water but an eternal, inner source — a spring that keeps giving. This is life in the Holy Spirit.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus' water wells up into forever.
verses.prayer: Jesus, give me Your living water. Fill me and let it spring up forever inside me.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus said His water becomes a spring inside us. What do you think that means?
verses.family_discussion: This verse is the memory verse for John 4. Talk about what it would mean for each family member to have a spring of living water inside them.
verses.do_it_today: Ask Jesus right now: 'Give me this living water.' Mean it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A person with a gentle glow at their chest — a spring of water bubbling up from inside them, reaching upward into light. Joy and fullness on their face. Living from within.

verses.kjv: The woman saith unto him, Sir, give me this water, that I thirst not, neither come hither to draw.
verses.little_bible: The woman said: 'Sir, please give me this water! Then I will never be thirsty again — and I will not have to keep coming here to draw water.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The woman said: 'Sir, give me this water so I never have to come here again!'
verses.meaning: She still heard it somewhat literally — but she was asking. Her request, even half-understood, was moving in the right direction. Jesus honours honest seeking.
verses.memory_phrase: Ask Jesus, even if you do not fully understand yet.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I want what You are offering — even the parts I do not fully understand yet. Please give it to me.
verses.discussion_question: The woman asked for something she only half understood. Do you think Jesus was pleased with her request? Why?
verses.family_discussion: God honours honest seeking — even imperfect, half-understanding asking. How does that encourage us to pray more boldly?
verses.do_it_today: Ask Jesus for something big today — even if you do not fully understand how He will do it.
verses.illustration_prompt: The woman leaning forward eagerly, hands reaching toward Jesus. Earnestness and hope on her face. The well behind her, already forgotten in her excitement.

verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto her, Go, call thy husband, and come hither.
verses.little_bible: Then Jesus said to her: 'Go and call your husband, and come back here.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Go and get your husband and come back.'
verses.meaning: An unexpected turn. Jesus was not being unkind — He was leading her toward honesty. True transformation requires truth. He needed to address what was real in her life.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus asks us to be honest before He transforms us.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me to be honest with You about my real life — not just the easy parts.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Jesus suddenly asked about her husband instead of just giving her the water?
verses.family_discussion: Sometimes God redirects a conversation to get to something real. Has God ever done that with your family — pointed to something that needed to be addressed first?
verses.do_it_today: Come before God with complete honesty today — including the part of your life you find hardest to talk about.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus with a calm, knowing look. The woman pauses mid-step, caught off guard. Something in his eyes tells her He already knows. A gentle but decisive moment.

verses.kjv: The woman answered and said, I have no husband. Jesus said unto her, Thou hast well said, I have no husband:
verses.little_bible: The woman answered: 'I have no husband.' Jesus said: 'You are right to say that — I have no husband.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The woman said: 'I have no husband.' Jesus said: 'That is true.'
verses.meaning: She told the truth as far as she went. Jesus did not shame her for a half-answer — He acknowledged what she said before gently completing the picture.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus meets truth with gentleness.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for not shaming me when I tell You the truth about my life.
verses.discussion_question: The woman told a partial truth. Jesus was kind to her anyway. What does that tell us about how Jesus responds to us?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus acknowledged what she said before revealing more. He did not condemn her. How does Jesus' gentleness in hard conversations model how we should talk with each other?
verses.do_it_today: Choose gentleness in a hard conversation today — especially when someone tells you only part of the truth.
verses.illustration_prompt: The woman meeting Jesus' eyes with a careful, guarded expression. Jesus nodding calmly, without surprise or judgment. A safe, honest moment.

verses.kjv: For thou hast had five husbands; and he whom thou now hast is not thy husband: in that saidst thou truly.
verses.little_bible: Jesus already knew everything about her life — even the sad and broken parts she hadn't told anyone. He knew, and He still spoke to her with kindness and respect.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus knew her whole story. He told her the truth gently and with kindness.
verses.meaning: Jesus knew everything and revealed it without cruelty. He was not exposing her to shame — He was showing her He saw her fully. Real love sees everything and still stays.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus knows everything about us — and still loves us.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You know everything about me — every mistake, every broken thing. And You still love me. Thank You.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus knew the woman's whole story — the sad and broken parts. How does it feel to know that Jesus knows everything about you and still wants to talk to you?
verses.family_discussion: Nothing is hidden from Jesus — and He loves us anyway. How does that change how we come to Him?
verses.do_it_today: Tell Jesus something about yourself today that you have been keeping quiet. He already knows — and He loves you.
verses.illustration_prompt: The woman's eyes wide with recognition — He knows. Jesus' expression is compassionate, not condemning. Light around them both. She is seen fully, and not destroyed by it.

verses.kjv: The woman saith unto him, Sir, I perceive that thou art a prophet.
verses.little_bible: The woman said: 'Sir, I can see that You are a prophet!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The woman said: 'I can see You are a prophet!'
verses.meaning: A significant moment — she moved from 'a Jew at a well' to 'a prophet.' Her understanding of Jesus was growing with every exchange. This is how encounters with Jesus work.
verses.memory_phrase: The more we see Jesus, the more we understand who He is.
verses.prayer: Jesus, reveal more of who You are to me each day.
verses.discussion_question: The woman's understanding of Jesus grew during their conversation. How has your understanding of who Jesus is grown over time?
verses.family_discussion: She started seeing Jesus as 'just a Jew' — and ended up seeing Him as the Messiah. How does our family's view of Jesus grow and deepen over time?
verses.do_it_today: Spend five minutes just thinking about who Jesus is — not what He does, but who He is.
verses.illustration_prompt: The woman stepping back slightly, a new expression on her face — wonder and dawning recognition. The veil of ordinary assumptions lifting. Something real happening.

verses.kjv: Our fathers worshipped in this mountain; and ye say, that in Jerusalem is the place where men ought to worship.
verses.little_bible: She said: 'Our ancestors worshipped God on this mountain — but you Jews say Jerusalem is the only right place to worship. Which one is right?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The woman asked: 'Our people worship on this mountain. You say Jerusalem is the right place. Who is right?'
verses.meaning: She raised the great theological controversy between Jews and Samaritans. She was a genuine seeker, not just deflecting. Jesus was about to transcend the debate entirely.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus has an answer that goes beyond the debate.
verses.prayer: God, help me bring my real questions to You — and trust that Your answer is better than any argument.
verses.discussion_question: The woman asked about a big religious debate. Do you think Jesus was bothered by big questions about religion? Why?
verses.family_discussion: She asked a big question about worship. Where and how does your family worship — and do you understand why?
verses.do_it_today: Write down one big question about God or religion that you have. Bring it to God in prayer.
verses.illustration_prompt: The woman pointing toward a distant mountain, with a questioning, sincere expression. Jesus listening without defensiveness — ready to give an answer that will change everything.

verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto her, Woman, believe me, the hour cometh, when ye shall neither in this mountain, nor yet at Jerusalem, worship the Father.
verses.little_bible: Jesus replied: 'Believe Me — the time is coming when people will not worship the Father on this mountain or in Jerusalem.',
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'A time is coming when worship will not be about mountains or cities.'
verses.meaning: Jesus did not take sides in the debate — He transcended it. The location of worship was about to become irrelevant. A new era was coming.
verses.memory_phrase: True worship is not about a place.
verses.prayer: Father, I worship You wherever I am — not because of a place, but because of who You are.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus said the place of worship would stop mattering. Do you think some people still think God is only in certain buildings or places?
verses.family_discussion: Worship does not require a specific building or location. Where do we encounter God outside of church?
verses.do_it_today: Worship God somewhere unusual today — outside, in the car, in the kitchen. Remind yourself that He is everywhere.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus gesturing with both hands wide open, as if clearing away the debate. The mountain behind the woman fades into the background. A new horizon visible ahead.

verses.kjv: Ye worship ye know not what: we know what we worship: for salvation is of the Jews.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'You Samaritans worship something you do not fully know. We Jews worship what we know — because salvation comes through the Jewish people.',
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Salvation comes through the Jewish people — that is why they worship what they know.'
verses.meaning: Jesus spoke truthfully — the Samaritans had an incomplete revelation of God. God's plan of salvation was being worked out through Israel and her scriptures. Jesus was that salvation.
verses.memory_phrase: God worked through Israel to bring salvation to all.
verses.prayer: God, thank You for working out Your plan of salvation through history — and bringing it to me.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus said salvation comes through the Jewish people. How does this connect to Jesus Himself being Jewish?
verses.family_discussion: The Bible's story of salvation runs through the nation of Israel — and leads to Jesus. How does knowing the Old Testament help us understand Jesus better?
verses.do_it_today: Read one Old Testament passage today and ask: 'How does this point to Jesus?'
verses.illustration_prompt: A golden thread running from ancient Hebrew scrolls, through the nation of Israel, arriving at the figure of Jesus. Salvation's long road.

verses.kjv: But the hour cometh, and now is, when the true worshippers shall worship the Father in spirit and in truth: for the Father seeketh such to worship him.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'The time is coming — and it has already arrived — when true worshippers will worship the Father in spirit and in truth. The Father is actually looking for people to worship Him that way.',
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'The Father wants people who worship Him from the heart with truth.'
verses.meaning: This is one of the most revolutionary statements about worship in Scripture. God does not want location-worship or ritual-worship; He seeks heart-worship — in spirit (genuine, Spirit-led) and in truth (according to who He really is).
verses.memory_phrase: God seeks worshippers in spirit and truth.
verses.prayer: Father, I want to be the kind of worshipper You are looking for. Help me to worship You truly from my heart.
verses.discussion_question: God is looking for worshippers. That means He wants you! What kind of worshipper do you want to be?
verses.family_discussion: What does worship 'in spirit and in truth' look like in practice — in your home, in your church, in your life?
verses.do_it_today: Worship God right now — not with words that sound right, but with what is truly in your heart.
verses.illustration_prompt: A figure worshipping with eyes closed, hands open — not in a grand building but in ordinary surroundings. A gentle glow around them. Spirit and truth made visible as light from within.

verses.kjv: God is a Spirit: and they that worship him must worship him in spirit and in truth.
verses.little_bible: God is Spirit — and those who worship Him must worship in spirit and in truth.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God is Spirit. We must worship Him from the heart with truth.
verses.meaning: Because God is Spirit — not confined to a place or a form — worship must match His nature. It must be spiritual (not just external ritual) and truthful (based on who God truly is).
verses.memory_phrase: God is Spirit — worship must come from within.
verses.prayer: God, You are Spirit — beyond all buildings and rituals. Teach me to meet You in that deep place.
verses.discussion_question: God is Spirit. What does that mean for how we connect with Him?
verses.family_discussion: External worship — going to church, singing songs — is good. But God wants what is on the inside too. How do we make sure our worship is truly from the heart?
verses.do_it_today: Do one act of worship today that nobody sees — just between you and God.
verses.illustration_prompt: Vast open space — sky, wind, light — representing God as Spirit. A small human figure below, worshipping. No buildings, no walls. Just God and His child in open relationship.

verses.kjv: The woman saith unto him, I know that Messias cometh, which is called Christ: when he is come, he will tell us all things.
verses.little_bible: The woman said: 'I know that the Messiah is coming — the one called Christ. When He comes, He will explain everything to us.',
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The woman said: 'I know the Messiah is coming. When He comes, He will explain everything.'
verses.meaning: She was waiting for the Messiah — even as a Samaritan, she held this hope. And she was speaking to Him right now without knowing it. The moment of revelation was one breath away.
verses.memory_phrase: She was looking for the Messiah — and He was right there.
verses.prayer: Jesus, open my eyes to see You when You are right in front of me.
verses.discussion_question: The woman was looking for the Messiah without realising she was already talking to Him. Does that ever happen to us — we look for something we already have?
verses.family_discussion: She was waiting for someone to 'explain everything.' Jesus was that person. How does Jesus — through the Bible and the Spirit — still explain things to us today?
verses.do_it_today: Come to the Bible today expecting Jesus to explain something to you — and be patient enough to hear it.
verses.illustration_prompt: The woman speaking earnestly, gesturing upward with hope. Jesus watching her, a gentle smile on His face — knowing that the moment she is waiting for is right now.

verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto her, I that speak unto thee am he.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said to her: 'I am He — the one speaking to you right now.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'I am the Messiah — I am speaking to you right now.'
verses.meaning: The greatest revelation in the chapter — and Jesus gave it first to a Samaritan woman. Not to the Pharisees, not to the disciples. To her. To the one who seemed to matter least.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus revealed Himself to her.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for revealing Yourself to me. I am amazed that You chose to show Yourself to me.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus told this woman He was the Messiah before He told many others. What does it tell us about Jesus that He revealed this to her?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus did not reveal Himself to the powerful — He revealed Himself to the outcast. What does that tell us about how God sees people?
verses.do_it_today: Think of someone who feels like they are on the outside — overlooked and forgotten. Jesus sees them. Can you let them know that today?
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus leaning forward slightly, meeting the woman's eyes directly. A moment of quiet, earth-changing revelation. Her face moving from curiosity to wonder to belief.
keywords: the one you are waiting for

verses.kjv: And upon this came his disciples, and they marvelled that he talked with the woman: yet no man said, What seekest thou? or, Why talkest thou with her?
verses.little_bible: Right then the disciples returned and were surprised to find Jesus talking with a woman. But none of them dared to ask: 'What do You want?' or 'Why are You talking with her?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The disciples came back and were surprised Jesus was talking to a woman — but no one said anything.
verses.meaning: The disciples' surprise reveals the cultural norm — teachers did not speak publicly with women. But they held back. Something in Jesus' bearing told them not to question it.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus broke the rules of His day — to include people.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me to follow You even when it means doing things that surprise others.
verses.discussion_question: The disciples were surprised but did not dare question Jesus. What do you think they were learning in that moment?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus regularly broke social expectations to include people. What expectations might He want our family to break to include others?
verses.do_it_today: Do something kind for someone today that others might find surprising.
verses.illustration_prompt: The disciples arriving back with food, stopping short as they see Jesus in deep, friendly conversation with the woman. Surprised but respectful. Something they will remember.

verses.kjv: The woman then left her waterpot, and went her way into the city, and saith to the men,
verses.little_bible: The woman was so overwhelmed that she left her water jar right there at the well and ran back into the city. She said to the people there:
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The woman left her water jar and ran back into the city to tell people.
verses.meaning: She left behind the very thing she came for — the water jar. She had found something infinitely better. The best news always makes you drop everything and run.
verses.memory_phrase: When you find Jesus, you run to tell others.
verses.prayer: Jesus, fill me with such joy in You that I cannot keep it to myself.
verses.discussion_question: She left her water jar behind — the whole reason she came! What does that show about how she felt after meeting Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: She was so excited about Jesus that she forgot everything else. When was a time your family was so excited about what God was doing that you had to tell someone?
verses.do_it_today: Tell someone today about something Jesus has done for you — even something small.
verses.illustration_prompt: A water jar sitting abandoned by the well. In the distance, the woman running back toward the city at full speed, leaving dust behind her. Joy and urgency.

verses.kjv: Come, see a man, which told me all things that ever I did: is not this the Christ?
verses.little_bible: She called out: 'Come and see a man who told me everything I have ever done! Could this be the Messiah — the Christ?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: She cried out: 'Come and see a man who told me everything I ever did! Is He the Messiah?'
verses.meaning: Her testimony was her story — 'He told me everything.' She did not hide her past; she used it. The thing she was most ashamed of became the proof of who Jesus was.
verses.memory_phrase: Your story is your testimony.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me to use my own story — even the hard parts — to tell others about You.
verses.discussion_question: The woman used her own story as her testimony. She shared the part of her life she might have been most ashamed of. Why is a personal story often a powerful way to share about Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: What is your family's testimony — the story of how God has worked in your lives? Who needs to hear it?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one part of your story where God has worked and look for an opportunity to share it today.
verses.illustration_prompt: The woman in the town square, arms outstretched, calling to townspeople with urgency and joy. Her face transformed from when she was at the well alone. She is alive with good news.

verses.kjv: Then they went out of the city, and came unto him.
verses.little_bible: The people of the town came out to see Jesus.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The people came out of the city to see Jesus.
verses.meaning: One woman's testimony moved a whole town. She was the most unlikely evangelist — yet God used her. She simply said what she knew, and people came.
verses.memory_phrase: One story brought a whole town to Jesus.
verses.prayer: God, use my story to bring others to You — just like You used hers.
verses.discussion_question: Just one woman's story brought a whole town out to see Jesus. What does that tell you about the power of one person sharing their experience?
verses.family_discussion: She did not have to be perfect, trained, or respected to bring people to Jesus. She just had a story. What stops your family from sharing yours?
verses.do_it_today: Share your story today — just as the woman did. Tell what Jesus has done for you.
verses.illustration_prompt: A stream of townspeople walking out of the city gates onto the open road, heading toward the well and the figure of Jesus in the distance. Movement and anticipation.

verses.kjv: In the mean while his disciples prayed him, saying, Master, eat.
verses.little_bible: Meanwhile, His disciples were urging Him: 'Teacher, please eat something!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The disciples said: 'Teacher, please eat!'
verses.meaning: The disciples were concerned about the physical — understandably. Jesus was about to redirect them to something greater, just as He had with the woman.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus was focused on something greater than food.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me to hunger for the things You care about most.
verses.discussion_question: The disciples wanted Jesus to eat. But Jesus had something more important on His mind. What do you think that was?
verses.family_discussion: We often focus on physical needs — food, rest, money. Jesus redirected His disciples to a different kind of hunger. What is your family truly hungry for?
verses.do_it_today: Spend time today on something that feeds your soul — not just your body.
verses.illustration_prompt: The disciples holding bread and fruit, looking concerned at a Jesus who seems energised without eating. He is looking toward the town in the distance — alive with purpose.

verses.kjv: But he said unto them, I have meat to eat that ye know not of.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'I have food to eat that you know nothing about.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'I have food you do not know about.'
verses.meaning: Again He spoke spiritually to those thinking physically. His 'food' was doing His Father's will — which He had just done with the Samaritan woman.
verses.memory_phrase: Doing God's will is food for the soul.
verses.prayer: Jesus, fill me with the hunger to do God's will — and let that be my deepest satisfaction.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus said He had food nobody knew about. What do you think He meant?
verses.family_discussion: When your family does what God calls you to do — serves, gives, shares — there is a deep satisfaction. Has your family experienced that?
verses.do_it_today: Do something that feeds your soul today — a good deed done for God's sake.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus with a satisfied, peaceful expression, eyes bright — more energised than if He had eaten. The disciples looking puzzled, checking if someone secretly gave Him food.

verses.kjv: Therefore said the disciples one to another, Hath any man brought him ought to eat?
verses.little_bible: The disciples whispered to each other: 'Did someone bring Him food without us knowing?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The disciples asked each other: 'Did someone bring Him food?'
verses.meaning: They still heard literally — just as the woman had. But this gives Jesus the opportunity to teach.
verses.memory_phrase: It takes time to understand the deeper things Jesus says.
verses.prayer: Jesus, when I do not understand what You mean, help me to stay curious instead of giving up.
verses.discussion_question: The disciples thought someone snuck Jesus a sandwich! What does this tell us about how easy it is to miss the spiritual meaning behind something?
verses.family_discussion: We often interpret things at face value. How do we learn to hear the deeper things Jesus is saying to us?
verses.do_it_today: Read one saying of Jesus today and ask: 'What is the deeper spiritual meaning here?'
verses.illustration_prompt: Three disciples huddled together, looking around suspiciously for a mystery food giver. One even checking under a cloth. Gently comic — and very human.

verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto them, My meat is to do the will of him that sent me, and to finish his work.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'My food — what truly sustains Me — is to do the will of the One who sent Me and to finish His work.',
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'My food is to do God's will and finish His work.'
verses.meaning: This is the core of Jesus' life and mission: not personal comfort, not fame — but completing the Father's work. This should be the model for every disciple's life.
verses.memory_phrase: Doing God's will was Jesus' deepest food.
verses.prayer: Father, make Your will my greatest hunger — the thing that truly sustains me.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus said doing God's will was like food to Him. What does that mean for how we should think about serving God?
verses.family_discussion: What is the 'work' God has sent your family to do? And are you finding the satisfaction in it that Jesus described?
verses.do_it_today: Say today: 'God, I want to live for Your will — not my own comfort.' Mean it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing tall and purposeful, looking toward the approaching townspeople. No need for physical food — He is sustained from within. The work of God energising Him.

verses.kjv: Say not ye, There are yet four months, and then cometh harvest? behold, I say unto you, Lift up your eyes, and look on the fields; for they are white already to harvest.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'You say there are four more months until harvest — but I tell you, look up and look around at the fields. They are already white and ready to be harvested right now!',
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Look! The fields are ready to harvest right now!'
verses.meaning: Jesus used the image of harvest fields — but He was pointing to the Samaritans now streaming toward them. The spiritual harvest was happening in real time. People ready for God are everywhere.
verses.memory_phrase: The harvest is ready — look up!
verses.prayer: God, open my eyes to see the people around me who are ready to hear about You.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus told the disciples to look up and see the harvest. What do you think the harvest is?
verses.family_discussion: The harvest is all the people ready to hear about Jesus. Who in your neighbourhood, school, or workplace might be 'ready to harvest'?
verses.do_it_today: Look up and around today. Who near you might be ready to hear about Jesus?
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus gesturing across the fields toward a crowd of Samaritans walking toward them — white robes in the distance like ripe grain. The disciples following His gaze with growing amazement.

verses.kjv: And he that reapeth receiveth wages, and gathereth fruit unto life eternal: that both he that soweth and he that reapeth may rejoice together.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'The one who harvests receives a great reward and gathers fruit for eternal life. Both the one who sows the seed and the one who reaps the harvest will be glad together.',
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'The one who harvests gets a great reward. The one who plants and the one who harvests can be happy together.'
verses.meaning: Gospel work is not competitive. Those who plant seeds and those who see the harvest rejoice together. All of it matters and all of it is rewarded.
verses.memory_phrase: Everyone who works in God's harvest rejoices together.
verses.prayer: God, help me to do my part in the harvest — whether I plant or reap — and to rejoice with everyone who does the same.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus said both the planter and the harvester rejoice together. Why is that important when it comes to sharing about Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: In bringing people to Jesus, some of us plant seeds and some of us see the fruit. Neither is more important. Has your family both planted seeds and seen a harvest?
verses.do_it_today: Celebrate someone today who has planted seeds for God — even if they never saw the harvest.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two farmers — one who planted, one who harvests — embracing joyfully in a golden field. Equal joy, equal honour. The fruit of shared labour.

verses.kjv: And herein is that saying true, One soweth, and another reapeth.
verses.little_bible: This confirms the saying: 'One person sows, another reaps.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The saying is true: one plants, another harvests.
verses.meaning: No single person does it all. God's work flows through many hands across many generations. We build on what others have done and others will build on what we do.
verses.memory_phrase: We all play a part in God's big story.
verses.prayer: God, help me to do my part faithfully — knowing others did theirs before me, and others will do theirs after me.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever benefited from something someone else worked hard to build? How does that make you feel about doing good work for those who come after you?
verses.family_discussion: Your family is part of a long line of people doing God's work. Who came before you, and who might benefit from what you plant now?
verses.do_it_today: Thank God for someone who planted seeds in your life — a parent, teacher, pastor, or friend.
verses.illustration_prompt: A timeline illustration: one person sowing seeds in an ancient field; further along, another person harvesting the same crop generations later. Connected across time.

verses.kjv: I sent you to reap that whereon ye bestowed no labour: other men laboured, and ye are entered into their labours.
verses.little_bible: Jesus told them: 'I am sending you to harvest a crop you did not plant. Others worked hard before you — and you are stepping into the fruit of their labour.',
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'You will harvest what others worked hard to plant.'
verses.meaning: The disciples would harvest what John the Baptist, the prophets, and the Scriptures had planted. We all reap where others have sown — and we sow where others will reap.
verses.memory_phrase: We stand on others' shoulders. Others will stand on ours.
verses.prayer: God, make me grateful for those who came before me. Help me to leave something good for those who come after.
verses.discussion_question: The disciples were going to harvest what others had worked to plant. Can you think of examples of this in real life — where we benefit from others' hard work?
verses.family_discussion: Your family has received blessings because of people who worked and sacrificed before you. How does recognising that change how you live?
verses.do_it_today: Name one person whose hard work made your life better. Take a moment to truly thank God for them.
verses.illustration_prompt: A disciple harvesting a ripe field, with an image behind them showing the generations who planted — prophets, parents, faithful people. A rich inheritance.

verses.kjv: And many of the Samaritans of that city believed on him for the saying of the woman, which testified, He told me all that ever I did.
verses.little_bible: Many of the Samaritans from that town believed in Jesus because of the woman's testimony. She had said: 'He told me everything I ever did.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Many Samaritans believed in Jesus because of the woman's testimony.
verses.meaning: The woman went from social outcast to key evangelist. Her simple testimony — 'He told me everything' — was enough to bring many to faith. God specialises in unlikely witnesses.
verses.memory_phrase: God uses unlikely people to bring others to faith.
verses.prayer: God, use me — just as I am — to bring others to faith in Jesus.
verses.discussion_question: The Samaritan woman was someone others might have looked down on. Yet God used her to bring a whole town to Jesus. What does that say about who God chooses to use?
verses.family_discussion: Has God ever used someone unexpected to bring your family closer to Him? Who was it?
verses.do_it_today: Never think you are too ordinary, too broken, or too unimportant for God to use. He used her — He will use you.
verses.illustration_prompt: A crowd of Samaritans gathered around Jesus at the well, listening intently. The woman standing at the edge of the group, watching — amazed at what her words set in motion.

verses.kjv: So when the Samaritans were come unto him, they besought him that he would tarry with them: and he abode there two days.
verses.little_bible: When the Samaritans reached Jesus, they begged Him to stay with them — and He stayed for two days.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The Samaritans asked Jesus to stay — and He stayed two days.
verses.meaning: Jesus stayed with the people that most Jews despised. He was not just passing through — He dwelt with them. He gave His time and presence to those who asked.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus stayed — because they asked.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I ask You to stay with me. Fill my home and my heart with Your presence.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus stayed two days with the Samaritans. Do you think that surprised people? What does His staying say about how He saw them?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus gave His time and presence to people who were considered outsiders. Is there someone on the margins of your community your family could give more time to?
verses.do_it_today: Invite the presence of Jesus into your home today. Ask Him to dwell with your family.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus sitting in the middle of a group of Samaritans — eating, talking, teaching. Fully present. People of all ages gathered around. No barriers. Warmth and belonging.

verses.kjv: And many more believed because of his own word;
verses.little_bible: And many more people believed — now because they heard Jesus speak for themselves.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Even more people believed after hearing Jesus speak for themselves.
verses.meaning: First they believed through the woman's testimony. Now they believed through direct encounter. Personal testimony brings people to Jesus; encounter with Jesus deepens the faith.
verses.memory_phrase: Hearing Jesus for yourself changes everything.
verses.prayer: Jesus, speak to me directly. I want to hear Your voice for myself.
verses.discussion_question: They first believed because of the woman's story — but then believed more deeply because of Jesus' own words. What is the difference?
verses.family_discussion: Other people's testimonies bring us to Jesus — but we each need a personal encounter with Him. Has each family member had a personal encounter with Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Spend time listening to Jesus today — reading His words in the Gospels and asking the Spirit to make them real to you.
verses.illustration_prompt: A close-up of faces in the crowd — eyes wide and attentive, listening to Jesus speak. Different from the earlier curiosity. Something deeper happening. Eyes glistening with belief.

verses.kjv: And said unto the woman, We believe, not because of thy saying: for we have heard him ourselves, and know that this is indeed the Christ, the Saviour of the world.
verses.little_bible: They told the woman: 'We believe now — not just because of what you told us. We have heard Him ourselves and we know: this truly is the Christ, the Saviour of the whole world!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The people said: 'We heard Jesus ourselves! He is the Saviour of the whole world!'
verses.meaning: One of the greatest confessions in the Gospel of John — 'the Saviour of the world.' Coming from Samaritans, who were outsiders to Israel, it was a foretaste of the global mission.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus is the Saviour of the whole world.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are the Saviour of the world — and my Saviour too. I believe.
verses.discussion_question: They called Jesus the 'Saviour of the world' — not just of the Jews, not just of Samaritans, but of the whole world. Who does that include?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus is the Saviour of the whole world — people of every nation, language, and background. How does your family reflect that global love in how you live and pray?
verses.do_it_today: Pray for someone from a different country or background today. Jesus died for them too.
verses.illustration_prompt: A diverse crowd looking up at Jesus — different faces, different clothes. One voice rising from the crowd: 'The Saviour of the world!' Joy and recognition on every face.

verses.kjv: Now after two days he departed thence, and went into Galilee.
verses.little_bible: After two days, Jesus left Samaria and continued on to Galilee.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: After two days, Jesus left and went to Galilee.
verses.meaning: Jesus moved on — the Samaritan chapter complete. He was always on the move, always on mission. A fire had been lit in Samaria; now He pressed forward.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus kept moving forward in mission.
verses.prayer: God, help me to complete each mission You give me — and then keep moving forward to the next.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus stayed two days and then moved on. How do we know when a season of work is complete and it is time to move on?
verses.family_discussion: Seasons of ministry come and go. What is the current season your family is in? And what might be the next?
verses.do_it_today: Thank God for the season you are in — and trust Him to show you when the next one begins.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus and His disciples walking away from Sychar, the well in the background. The townspeople watching from a distance — changed forever. A new road ahead.

verses.kjv: For Jesus himself testified, that a prophet hath no honour in his own country.
verses.little_bible: Jesus Himself had said that a prophet is not honoured in his own hometown.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said that a prophet is not respected in his own hometown.
verses.meaning: Familiarity breeds contempt — those who knew Jesus as a boy would struggle to receive Him as the Messiah. He was heading somewhere where this tension was real.
verses.memory_phrase: Familiarity can blind us to who Jesus really is.
verses.prayer: Jesus, do not let familiarity make me take You for granted. Let me see You fresh every day.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think people who grow up around someone often struggle to see how special they are?
verses.family_discussion: Do we ever take the things of God for granted because we have heard them so many times? How do we keep our hearts fresh toward Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Read a familiar story about Jesus today as if for the very first time. Let it surprise you.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus approaching a familiar town. Faces that recognise Him but look uncertain — seeing not the Messiah but the carpenter's son. The tension of familiarity.

verses.kjv: Then when he was come into Galilee, the Galilaeans received him, having seen all the things that he did at Jerusalem at the feast: for they also went unto the feast.
verses.little_bible: But when He arrived in Galilee, the Galileans welcomed Him — they had seen the miracles He did in Jerusalem at the festival, since many of them had been there too.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The Galileans welcomed Jesus because they had seen His miracles in Jerusalem.
verses.meaning: Welcome, but on the basis of miracles — not deep faith. The narrator subtly notes this. Signs can attract crowds without producing true disciples.
verses.memory_phrase: Not all welcome is deep faith.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I do not want to just admire Your miracles — I want to truly know and follow You.
verses.discussion_question: The Galileans welcomed Jesus because of the miracles they had seen. Is that a good reason? What kind of welcome does Jesus really want?
verses.family_discussion: People can be impressed by God's power without truly surrendering to Him. What is the difference between admiring Jesus and following Him?
verses.do_it_today: Ask yourself: 'Am I following Jesus or just admiring what He can do for me?'
verses.illustration_prompt: Excited Galileans greeting Jesus as He arrives — cheerful, buzzing with news of the Jerusalem miracles. Jesus looking into their hearts with quiet wisdom. He knows what is there.

verses.kjv: So Jesus came again into Cana of Galilee, where he made the water wine. And there was a certain nobleman, whose son was sick at Capernaum.
verses.little_bible: Jesus returned to Cana in Galilee — the town where He had turned water into wine. And there was a royal official whose son was seriously ill in Capernaum.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus came back to Cana, where He turned water to wine. A royal official's son was very sick.
verses.meaning: The scene is set for the second miracle in Galilee. John links it to the first Cana miracle — both signs pointing to Jesus' power and identity.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus came back — and a miracle was waiting.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I am waiting for You to come into a situation in my life that needs Your power.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the Gospel records where Jesus had been before? What does that tell us about Jesus' ministry?
verses.family_discussion: Is there a situation in your family right now where someone is sick or struggling? What does it mean that Jesus — the One who worked miracles — is with you?
verses.do_it_today: Bring the name of a sick or struggling person to God in prayer right now.
verses.illustration_prompt: Cana's village in soft evening light — a place of memories. In the foreground, a man in fine clothing approaches — urgency in his stride. His son is dying and Jesus is here.

verses.kjv: When he heard that Jesus was come out of Judaea into Galilee, he went unto him, and besought him that he would come down, and heal his son: for he was at the point of death.
verses.little_bible: When the official heard Jesus had come to Galilee, he travelled to find Him and begged Him to come back to Capernaum to heal his son — the boy was dying.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The official heard Jesus was nearby and begged Him to come and heal his dying son.
verses.meaning: A father's desperation drove him to Jesus — status and wealth meant nothing now. Only Jesus could help. Great need pushes people past pride.
verses.memory_phrase: Desperation drives us to Jesus — and that is okay.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I come to You with my desperate need. I believe You can help.
verses.discussion_question: The nobleman came to Jesus because his son was dying — he was desperate. Does desperation make it harder or easier to come to Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: The man was important and wealthy — but none of that helped. Only Jesus could. Has your family experienced a moment of deep need that drove you to Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Come to Jesus today with your most desperate need — nothing hidden, nothing held back.
verses.illustration_prompt: A man in fine official robes — but undignified in his urgency — kneeling before Jesus with tears in his eyes. Status does not matter now. His son is dying.

verses.kjv: Then said Jesus unto him, Except ye see signs and wonders, ye will not believe.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said to him: 'You people will not believe unless you see miraculous signs and wonders.',
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'You will not believe unless you see miracles.'
verses.meaning: Jesus challenged the crowd's sign-dependence — but not the man's faith. He was pressing the father toward a deeper kind of trust — faith without needing to see first.
verses.memory_phrase: True faith trusts before it sees.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me to believe You without needing to see a miracle first.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus said some people only believe when they see miracles. Is that a good or bad way to believe? Why?
verses.family_discussion: Faith that depends only on miracles is fragile. What kind of faith does Jesus want from us?
verses.do_it_today: Trust Jesus today with something — before you see the answer. That is what faith looks like.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus looking at the crowd around the official, then back at the man himself. A searching look — not unkind, but probing. What kind of faith do you have?

verses.kjv: The nobleman saith unto him, Sir, come down ere my child die.
verses.little_bible: The official said: 'Sir — please come down before my child dies!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The man cried: 'Please come before my child dies!'
verses.meaning: The father did not argue with Jesus — he just kept asking. His persistence was pure love. Sometimes the right response to Jesus is simply: keep asking.
verses.memory_phrase: Keep asking. Do not give up.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I keep asking. I do not stop. Please come into my situation.
verses.discussion_question: The nobleman did not argue with Jesus or get offended — he just kept asking. What does that show about real faith?
verses.family_discussion: Has your family ever kept praying for something despite delays or challenges? What did that do to your faith?
verses.do_it_today: Pray again today for something you have been praying about for a long time. Do not give up.
verses.illustration_prompt: The official leaning toward Jesus, voice cracking with urgency. 'Before my child dies.' Pure fatherly love stripped of all pride. Jesus hearing every word.

verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto him, Go thy way; thy son liveth. And the man believed the word that Jesus had spoken unto him, and he went his way.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said to him: 'You can go. Your son is alive.' And the man believed what Jesus said — and started walking home.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Go! Your son will live.' The man believed Jesus and went home.
verses.meaning: Jesus healed from a distance, with a word alone. No travel, no touch, no ceremony. And the man believed before he saw any evidence. This is saving faith — taking Jesus at His word.
verses.memory_phrase: The man believed Jesus' word and walked.
verses.prayer: Jesus, give me the faith to take You at Your word — to trust Your promise before I see the proof.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus did not go with the man — He just said 'your son lives.' The man believed and walked home. What kind of faith does that take?
verses.family_discussion: The man took Jesus at His word without any sign — and walked twenty miles home trusting that promise. Is there a promise of God your family needs to walk in right now?
verses.do_it_today: Find one promise from God in the Bible. Write it down. Walk in it today as if it is already done.
verses.illustration_prompt: The official turning and walking away — not running, but steady and purposeful. No miracle seen yet, but faith written on his face. The word of Jesus was enough.

verses.kjv: And as he was now going down, his servants met him, and told him, saying, Thy son liveth.
verses.little_bible: As he was on his way home, his servants met him with news: 'Your son is alive!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: On the way home, his servants ran to him: 'Your son is alive!'
verses.meaning: The confirmation came on the road — as he was walking in faith. The good news met him in his obedience. That is often how God works.
verses.memory_phrase: Confirmation came while he was walking in faith.
verses.prayer: God, help me to keep walking in faith — and trust that the confirmation will come.
verses.discussion_question: The servants met the man while he was still on his way home — still walking by faith. How does that encourage you when you are waiting for an answer?
verses.family_discussion: God often confirms His answers to us while we are in the middle of obeying, not before. Has your family experienced something like this?
verses.do_it_today: Keep walking in what God has called you to. The confirmation is on its way.
verses.illustration_prompt: A dusty road through hills. The official walking purposefully — and then seeing his servants running toward him in the distance. Their arms waving, good news written in their bodies.

verses.kjv: Then enquired he of them the hour when he began to amend. And they said unto him, Yesterday at the seventh hour the fever left him.
verses.little_bible: He asked them what time the boy had started to get better. They told him: 'Yesterday at one o'clock in the afternoon, the fever left him.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: He asked when his son got better. They said: 'Yesterday at one o'clock — the fever left!'
verses.meaning: He was a careful man — he wanted the exact time. The confirmation he received was precise and undeniable. Jesus' miracles are specific and verifiable.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus' miracles are real and specific.
verses.prayer: Jesus, Your work in my life is real — I want to notice and record the specific ways You move.
verses.discussion_question: The man asked for the exact time. Why do you think he wanted to know exactly when his son was healed?
verses.family_discussion: God's works are specific and real — not vague or general. Does your family record the specific ways God has worked? A prayer journal can help.
verses.do_it_today: Write down one specific answer to prayer you have seen. Be precise — what, when, and how God answered.
verses.illustration_prompt: The official pausing, eyes widening — asking the exact time. The servant's face holding up fingers: the seventh hour. A precise moment being mapped against a divine act.

verses.kjv: So the father knew that it was at the same hour, in the which Jesus said unto him, Thy son liveth: and himself believed, and his whole house.
verses.little_bible: The father realised that the exact hour when Jesus had said 'your son is alive' was the same moment the fever broke. And he believed — and so did his whole household.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The father knew it was exactly when Jesus spoke — and he believed, and his whole family believed too.
verses.meaning: The miracle confirmed what faith had already grasped. And the result was not just one man's faith — it was a whole household brought to belief. Faith is contagious.
verses.memory_phrase: His whole house believed.
verses.prayer: Jesus, let my faith spread to my whole family and household. Let no one be left outside.
verses.discussion_question: The father believed first — and then his whole household believed. How does the faith of one person affect a whole family?
verses.family_discussion: Someone in your family probably came to faith first. How did that faith spread? And is there still someone in your wider household who has not yet believed?
verses.do_it_today: Pray for every person in your household by name — that each one would believe in Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: A joyful household — servants, family members, all gathered together in the light. The father in the centre, face radiant. One miracle touching every life in the home.

verses.kjv: This is again the second miracle that Jesus did, when he was come out of Judaea into Galilee.
verses.little_bible: This was the second miracle Jesus performed after coming from Judaea into Galilee.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: This was the second miracle Jesus did after coming from Judaea to Galilee.
verses.meaning: John carefully numbers Jesus' signs — this is sign number two in Galilee. Each miracle is a revelation, a window into who Jesus is. The signs are building to a bigger picture.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus' miracles are signs pointing to who He is.
verses.prayer: Jesus, every miracle You do is a sign showing me who You are. Help me to see You more clearly.
verses.discussion_question: John carefully counts the miracles of Jesus as 'signs.' What are they signs of? What do they point to?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus' miracles were not just amazing acts — they were signs pointing to His identity. Which miracle of Jesus speaks most powerfully to your family about who He is?
verses.do_it_today: Read about one miracle of Jesus today and ask: 'What does this tell me about who Jesus is?'
verses.illustration_prompt: A quiet coda scene: the number '2' written in glowing characters over a background of Galilee's hills. Two signs, two stories, both pointing to one truth — Jesus is the Son of God.
